{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981","id":2565206585,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6Y5fI5","number":55981,"title":"Implement hyperbolic version of sinc: sinhc (feature request).","user":{"login":"dtabell","id":11483676,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNDgzNjc2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11483676?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell","html_url":"https://github.com/dtabell","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-04T00:38:54Z","updated_at":"2024-10-04T00:38:54Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"_Julia_ defines the function $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ using the _normalized_ form $\sin(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
Replacing $x$ by $\mathrm{i}x$ yields the new function $\sinh(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
Because this function also (annoyingly) has a singularity at the origin,
I suggest implementing it as a new special function named $\mathrm{sinhc}$.

The notation $\mathrm{sinhc}$ has already been suggested, see [_Wolfram MathWorld_](https://mathworld.wolfram.com/SinhcFunction.html),
for the cognate function $\mathrm{sinh}(x)/x$, which is consistent with its definition
of $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ using the _unnormalized_ form $\sin(x)/x$. The same (unnormalized)
version appears also in the [boost C++ special functions library](https://www.boost.org/doc/libs/1_86_0/boost/math/special_functions/sinhc.hpp), and uses
the same notation.

I could, of course, implement this function myself, but it _does_ seem the sort of
function that merits inclusion in the standard set of _Julia_ special functions.
Please let me know if the _Julia_ team is already at work extending the library of
special functions, or if I should implement this function and issue a pull request.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

requests"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-10-04T00:38:54Z","updated_at":"2024-10-07T17:20:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"_Julia_ defines the function $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ using the _normalized_ form $\sin(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
Replacing $x$ by $\mathrm{i}x$ yields the new function $\sinh(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
Because this function also (annoyingly) has a singularity at the origin,
I suggest implementing it as a new special function named $\mathrm{sinhc}$.

The notation $\mathrm{sinhc}$ has already been suggested, see [_Wolfram MathWorld_](https://mathworld.wolfram.com/SinhcFunction.html),
for the cognate function $\mathrm{sinh}(x)/x$, which is consistent with its definition
of $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ using the _unnormalized_ form $\sin(x)/x$. The same (unnormalized)
version appears also in the [boost C++ special functions library](https://www.boost.org/doc/libs/1_86_0/boost/math/special_functions/sinhc.hpp), and uses
the same notation.

I could, of course, implement this function myself, but it _does_ seem the sort of
function that merits inclusion in the standard set of _Julia_ special functions.
Please let me know if the _Julia_ team is already at work extending the library of
special functions, or if I should implement this function and issue a pull request.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2397485407","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981#issuecomment-2397485407","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55981","id":2397485407,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6O5rlf","user":{"login":"dtabell","id":11483676,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjExNDgzNjc2","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11483676?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell","html_url":"https://github.com/dtabell","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dtabell/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-07T17:20:44Z","updated_at":"2024-10-07T17:20:44Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"My understanding is that for $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ mathematicians tend to prefer the definition $\sin(x)/x$,
whereas engineers—especially for signal processing—tend to prefer $\sin(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
I don't see a reason to favor one community over the other, so perhaps supporting both
makes sense. And of course a prominent warning would need to be emitted for at least a
few releases, or until v.2.0.

And then have corresponding versions for $\mathrm{sinhc}$.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2397485407/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

defines the function $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ using the _normalized_ form $\sin(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
Replacing $x$ by $\mathrm{i}x$ yields the new function $\sinh(\pi x)/(\pi x)$.
Because this function also (annoyingly) has a singularity at the origin,
I suggest implementing it as a new special function named $\mathrm{sinhc}$.

The notation $\mathrm{sinhc}$ has already been suggested, see [_Wolfram MathWorld_](https://mathworld.wolfram.com/SinhcFunction.html),
for the cognate function $\mathrm{sinh}(x)/x$, which is consistent with its definition
of $\mathrm{sinc}(x)$ using the _unnormalized_ form $\sin(x)/x$. The same (unnormalized)
version appears also in the [boost C++ special functions library](https://www.boost.org/doc/libs/1_86_0/boost/math/special_functions/sinhc.hpp), and uses
the same notation.

I could, of course, implement this function myself, but it _does_ seem the sort of
function that merits inclusion in the standard set of _Julia_ special functions.
Please let me know if the _Julia_ team is already at work extending the library of
special functions, or if I should implement this function and issue a pull
